question_text: A red object appears red in white light because...

options.label: A
options.text: It absorbs all colours of light except red, which it reflects

options.label: B
options.text: It produces red light from within itself

options.label: C
options.text: It absorbs red light and reflects all other colours

options.label: D
options.text: Red light has a longer wavelength so it passes through better

explanation: Objects appear coloured because they absorb certain wavelengths and reflect others. A red object absorbs all colours except red, which it reflects to our eyes. This is how we perceive its colour.
